- Player is back at rows 8-12, cols 14-18 (starting position)
- No gray nodes (the chamber at cols 20-24 is now a black wall)

This is a key insight: SPACE resets everything to the initial state. The player starts fresh each time SPACE is pressed.

  - Black wall at cols 38-39
  - Red/blue at cols 40-41

- A horizontal corridor at rows 8-12, with the player at cols 14-18
- A white passage at rows 14-18, cols 19-23
- A white passage at rows 14-18, cols 31-37
- Red path at cols 40-41, running vertically
The goal is at rows 49-55, cols 43-49 (bottom-right).

- Chambers (blue) that the player can occupy
- Walls (black) that block movement
- Passages (white) that connect chambers

- Rows 14-18, cols 14-18: appears when player moves DOWN after SPACE (blue)

But SPACE resets the maze. So the player can only make one move before SPACE resets everything.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the sequence:
1. Initial: Player at rows 8-12, cols 14-18
2. RIGHT: Player moved to rows 8-12, cols 20-24
3. LEFT: Player moved back to rows 8-12, cols 14-18
4. SPACE: Toggled to vertical mode. Player still at rows 8-12, cols 14-18.
5. DOWN: Player moved to rows 14-18, cols 14-18

7. RIGHT: Player moved to rows 8-12, cols 20-24
8. SPACE: Toggled to vertical mode. Player reset to rows 8-12, cols 14-18.
9. DOWN: Player moved to rows 14-18, cols 14-18
10. RIGHT: No movement (no chamber to the right)
11. UP: Player moved to rows 8-12, cols 14-18
12. RIGHT: Player moved to rows 8-12, cols 20-24
13. SPACE: Reset to rows 8-12, cols 14-18
So the player can make multiple moves without pressing SPACE. The player only needs to press SPACE to toggle between horizontal and vertical modes.
But wait, after step 12 (RIGHT), the player was at rows 8-12, cols 20-24. After step 13 (SPACE), the player was reset to rows 8-12, cols 14-18. So SPACE always resets the player to the starting position.
The key question is: can the player make multiple moves without pressing SPACE? Let me check.
After step 2 (RIGHT), the player was at rows 8-12, cols 20-24. The player didn't press SPACE, so the player was still in horizontal mode. Then the player pressed LEFT (step 3) and moved back to rows 8-12, cols 14-18.
